display.purpose: Hero headlines and large page titles. At most one per screen.

headline.purpose: Section titles, modal titles, major nav labels.

title.purpose: Card titles, list-item titles, subsection headers.

body.purpose: Default running text. 15px between dense and editorial; cap at 65–75ch in reading surfaces.

label.purpose: Form-field labels, compact button text, small caps-style labels.

motion.name: ease-state
motion.purpose: Default easing for state transitions (hover, focus, active).

motion.name: duration-fast
motion.purpose: Color and background transitions on interactive elements.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The canonical primary affordance. Light periwinkle on near-black text; load-bearing inversion.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #c3c7fc; color: #181818; font-family: 'Borna', sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.025em; padding: 0.5rem 1rem; border: none; border-radius: 0.25rem; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #a6acfb; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #c3c7fc; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { background: #62659d; color: #3f3d3c; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Surface-matched secondary action. Subtle border, used when two primary buttons would compete.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { background: #181818; color: #ffffff; font-family: 'Borna', sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.025em; padding: 0.5rem 1rem; border: 1px solid #2a2a29; border-radius: 0.25rem; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1), color 150ms; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: #2a2a29; color: #cfcac5; border-color: #181818; } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #c3c7fc; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Outline Button
components.description: Promoted secondary action in the brand voice. Periwinkle border and text, transparent surface.
components.css: .ds-btn-outline { background: transparent; color: #c3c7fc; font-family: 'Borna', sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.025em; padding: 0.5rem 1rem; border: 1px solid #c3c7fc; border-radius: 0.25rem; cursor: pointer; transition: color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1), border-color 150ms; } .ds-btn-outline:hover { color: #a6acfb; border-color: #a6acfb; background: transparent; } .ds-btn-outline:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #c3c7fc; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Danger Button
components.description: Destructive confirmation. Solid red, full-contrast text. Reserved for actions like delete, revoke, force-deploy.
components.css: .ds-btn-danger { background: #dd5151; color: #ffffff; font-family: 'Borna', sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.025em; padding: 0.5rem 1rem; border: 1px solid #dd5151; border-radius: 0.25rem; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-btn-danger:hover { background: #4a1b1b; }

components.name: Input Field
components.description: Text input. Surface matches primary, focus ring is the brand periwinkle.
components.css: .ds-input-wrap { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 0.375rem; font-family: 'Borna', sans-serif; } .ds-input-label { font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.025em; color: #e9e3dd; } .ds-input { background: #181818; color: #ffffff; font-family: 'Borna', sans-serif; font-size: 0.9375rem; line-height: 1.4; padding: 0.5rem 0.75rem; border: 1px solid #2a2a29; border-radius: 0.25rem; outline: none; transition: border-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); width: 100%; box-sizing: border-box; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #84807d; } .ds-input:focus-visible { border-color: #c3c7fc; box-shadow: 0 0 0 1px #c3c7fc; }

components.name: Card
components.description: Content container. One tonal step above the page, no shadow, optional 1px border.
components.html:
typescript-good-practices

v2.3 · deployed to 14 repos

Standard governing error classes, DTO intersections, and presentation enrichment. Owned by the Platform team.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #181818; color: #ffffff; font-family: 'Borna', sans-serif; padding: 1rem; border: 1px solid #2a2a29; border-radius: 0.25rem; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 0.5rem; max-width: 520px; } .ds-card-title { font-size: 1.25rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.4; margin: 0; color: #ffffff; } .ds-card-meta { font-size: 0.8125rem; line-height: 1.3; margin: 0; color: #84807d; } .ds-card-body { font-size: 0.9375rem; line-height: 1.4; margin: 0; color: #e9e3dd; max-width: 65ch; }

components.name: Table Row
components.description: Governance-list row. Comfortable density, tonal background, no shadow.
components.css: .ds-table { width: 100%; border-collapse: collapse; font-family: 'Borna', sans-serif; font-size: 0.9375rem; color: #ffffff; } .ds-table thead th { background: #181818; color: #ffffff; font-weight: 500; font-size: 0.875rem; letter-spacing: 0.025em; text-align: left; padding: 0.75rem 1rem; border-bottom: 1px solid #2a2a29; } .ds-table tbody td { background: #2a2a29; padding: 0.75rem 1rem; border-bottom: 1px solid #181818; line-height: 1.4; } .ds-table tbody tr:nth-child(even) td { background: #3f3d3c; } .ds-table tbody tr:hover td { background: #555352; }

components.name: Subtle Chip
components.description: Quiet tag, used for category, framework, or owner labels in lists.
components.css: .ds-chip { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; background: #3f3d3c; color: #cfcac5; font-family: 'Borna', sans-serif; font-size: 0.8125rem; font-weight: 500; padding: 0.125rem 0.5rem; border-radius: 0.125rem; line-height: 1.3; } .ds-chip-success { background: #304328; color: #dde9d9; } .ds-chip-error { background: #6c2727; color: #ffdddd; }

components.name: Nav Item
components.description: Primary navigation entry. Underline-style active state, periwinkle accent.
components.html: StandardsCommandsSkillsDeployments
components.css: .ds-nav { display: flex; gap: 0; background: #181818; border-bottom: 1px solid #2a2a29; font-family: 'Borna', sans-serif; } .ds-nav-item { color: #b6b1ad; font-size: 0.9375rem; font-weight: 400; text-decoration: none; padding: 0.75rem 1rem; border-bottom: 2px solid transparent; margin-bottom: -1px; transition: color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1), border-color 150ms; } .ds-nav-item:hover { color: #ffffff; } .ds-nav-active { color: #ffffff; border-bottom-color: #c3c7fc; }

narrative.overview: Packmind's design system is a calm, warm-dark surface that reads like a curator's workshop after hours: aged-paper neutrals tinted with warmth, a single periwinkle accent that behaves like ink under lamplight, and almost no chrome between the reader and the content. The product's job is to make a team's engineering playbook coherent across many AI agents; the interface stays out of the way of that work. Density is earned in the governance surfaces (lists, version history, deployment status) and relaxed in the editing surfaces (writing a standard, reviewing a change). This system explicitly rejects the enterprise-B2B-admin default and the AI-startup-template look. The aesthetic lane is Notion + Vercel dashboard, leaning Vercel: strong typography, content-led layout, tonal depth without shadows.
keyCharacteristics: Warm-dark surface as the default. beige-1000 (#181818) is the page, not pure black.
Single brand accent. blue-200 periwinkle, used sparingly on primary affordances.
Single typeface. Borna carries every weight; no display/body pairing.
Flat at rest. Depth is conveyed through four tonal background steps, never through shadow.
Quiet, low-chrome components. Borders are subtle, fills are deliberate, decoration is absent.

rules.name: The One Accent Rule
rules.body: blue-200 periwinkle is the only chromatic identity color. It carries primary affordance, focus, and link state. Do not introduce a second brand color, do not stack purples next to it, and do not promote orange or yellow to brand status.

rules.name: The Warm-Neutral Rule
rules.body: Neutrals are beige, not gray. Every background or border choice resolves to a beige-* token. Cool slate grays are forbidden; they break the workshop warmth.

rules.name: The Inverted-Primary Rule
rules.body: The primary button is light on dark: blue-200 background, beige-1000 text. The inversion is what makes the brand visible against a dark page.

rules.name: The Single-Voice Rule
rules.body: Borna does all the work. Do not pair it with a display serif, a mono accent face for headlines, or a secondary sans.

rules.name: The Weight-and-Scale Rule
rules.body: Hierarchy is established through scale and weight contrast, never through color. A title is not 'the blue one'; it is the larger, heavier one.

rules.name: The 65–75ch Rule
rules.body: Reading surfaces cap body line length at 65–75ch. Governance lists and tables are exempt; density is the point there.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: No element is elevated at rest. No box-shadow on cards, panels, headers, or tabs. If a surface needs to feel above another, change its tonal step on the beige ramp.

rules.name: The Tonal-Depth Rule
rules.body: Stack at most three tonal steps in any single composition. More than three steps reads as visual noise and collapses the hierarchy.

rules.name: The Overlay Exception
rules.body: Modals, menus, toasts, and combobox popovers may use a single subtle drop-shadow to detach from the page beneath. Treat this as an exception, not a vocabulary.

dos: Do use blue-200 (#c3c7fc) as the single brand accent: on primary buttons, focus rings, outline buttons, and active-nav indicators.
Do use the inverted primary button (light periwinkle on near-black text) as the canonical primary affordance.
Do convey depth through the four-step beige-1000 → beige-900 → beige-800 → beige-700 ramp, with at most three steps stacked in one composition.
Do keep all text in the neutral ramp (beige-0 → beige-500) for body and labels; reserve color for state.
Do use Borna for every hierarchy step; vary scale and weight, not family.
Do cap body line length at 65–75ch in reading surfaces (artifact editor, change review, docs panels).
Do prefer inline disclosure and side-panels to modals; modals are reserved for genuinely disruptive flows.
Do surface governance state (version, owner, deployment, adoption) in the primary view, not behind a History tab.
donts: Don't use cramped gray-on-gray data tables, modal-for-everything flows, or tab-and-form-grid configuration panels: the enterprise-B2B-admin default.
Don't introduce purple gradients, hero-metric tiles, glassmorphism, identical card grids, or AI-sparkle iconography.
Don't use developer-cool maximalism: neon terminals, scroll-triggered animations, 3D logos.
Don't use pure #000 or #fff for surfaces. Backgrounds are warm beige-*.
Don't use cool slate grays as neutrals. Neutrals are beige.
Don't add box-shadow to cards, panels, table rows, headers, or tabs. Shadows live only on true overlays.
Don't use border-left or border-right greater than 1px as a colored accent stripe on cards, alerts, or list items. Never.
Don't apply background-clip: text with a gradient. Use a solid color; emphasize with weight or size.
Don't pair Borna with a serif display face, a mono headline, or a secondary sans. One voice.
Don't introduce a second brand color. Periwinkle is the only chromatic identity.
Don't rely on color alone to convey status. Pair every semantic color with text, icon, or position.
Don't use em dashes in UI copy. Commas, colons, semicolons, periods, parentheses. Also not '--'.
